data.title: Stocks with high correlation to interest rates reached all-time high in March
data.description:
Companies whose stock prices have historically shown high correlation to movement in interest rates recently saw an all-time high as odds of rate hikes may seem more plausible for investors than rate cuts.

Although it has lagged in the past week, the ProShares Equities for Rising Rates ETF (EQRR) saw an all-time high on March 24, reaching $71.

Year-to-date, the ETF is up 7.3% and up 16.2% from a year ago.

According to the CME FedWatch Tool, traders are pricing in 99.5% odds of interest rates staying the same at 3.50%-3.75%, no chance of a rate cut, and about 0.5% of a rate hike.

 

 

Here are the top 10 holdings of the EQRR ETF and their year-to-date performances:

  1. Baker Hughes Co. (BKR) +31.9% YTD
  2. Occidental Petroleum Corp. (OXY) +52.9% YTD
  3. Halliburton Co. (HAL) +34.9% YTD
  4. Valero Energy Corp. (VLO) +49% YTD
  5. Chevron Corp. (CVX) +30.6% YTD
  6. ConocoPhillips (COP) +38.5% YTD
  7. Devon Energy Corp. (DVN) +33.8% YTD
  8. EOG Resources (EOG) +34.9% YTD
  9. Diamondback Energy (FANG) +29.1% YTD
  10. Coterra Energy (CTRA) +30.2% YTD

data.title: Artemis II crew begins precision burn to set course for moon flyby
data.description:
A four-person crew from NASA is entering a pivotal phase of its mission that will send them toward the moon, marking humanity’s closest approach to the lunar surface in over half a century.

The Lockheed Martin-built (LMT) Orion spacecraft is scheduled to fire its main engine Thursday evening in a maneuver known as a translunar injection burn. This carefully timed thrust will place the capsule on the correct path toward the moon, leaving little margin for error. The burn comes on the second day of the mission’s planned 10-day journey.

The Artemis II crew lifted off Wednesday from Kennedy Space Center in Florida, beginning a mission designed to test systems ahead of future lunar landings.

If all goes according to plan, the astronauts will spend about four days traveling to the moon’s vicinity, with a close flyby expected on April 6. The mission represents a major milestone for NASA after years of development and billions in investment, as the agency works toward establishing a long-term human presence on the lunar surface.

Although Artemis II will not attempt a landing, it is a key step in NASA’s broader roadmap, which aims to return astronauts to the moon later this decade.

At their closest point, the spacecraft is expected to pass roughly 4,100 miles (6,598.31 km) from the moon. From the crew’s vantage point, the lunar surface will appear about the size of a basketball held at arm’s length.

The mission could also set a new record for the farthest distance humans have traveled from Earth, surpassing the crew of Apollo 13 mission.

Commanding the flight is astronaut Reid Wiseman, joined by pilot Victor Glover and mission specialist Christina Koch. Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen completes the crew.

During the journey, the astronauts will carry out a series of tests and experiments, including preparations to capture images of the moon during the flyby. The crew is also expected to communicate with astronauts aboard the International Space Station in what would be a first-of-its-kind exchange for NASA during a deep-space mission.

data.title: Vertiv Holdings tops large-cap ROE growth list, as market prepares to ride out volatility
data.description:
Higher rates are a growing headwind for stocks: the higher they go, the more pressure they put on equities. After a month of conflict, strategists are likely to start lowering year-end targets as oil prices keep inflation fears alive and the market stays focused on just one big issue.

For now, the best approach is to ride out the volatility by owning stocks with strong company-specific strength, experts suggest. From a quant view, that means names with high return on equity and solid EPS growth.

Below is a list of the top 10 large cap stocks ranked by their year over year ROE growth. The list includes market capitalization data and EPS revision grades for each stock.

The list is topped by Vertiv Holdings Co (VRT), with a year over year ROE growth of 87.59%. AT&T Inc. (T) and Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) follow closely behind, with Constellation Brands, Inc. (STZ) and Equinix, Inc. (EQIX) rounding out the top five.

The top ten spans a diverse range of industries, from electrical components and telecommunications to semiconductors and data center REITs. Nasdaq, Inc. (NDAQ), MKS Inc. (MKSI), and Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. (KDP) are among the other top performers, along with US Foods Holding Corp. (USFD) and Keysight Technologies, Inc. (KEYS).

Here is the list:

  1. Vertiv Holdings Co (VRT), YOY ROE growth: 87.59%, EPS revision: A+

  2. AT&T Inc. (T), YOY ROE growth: 83.90%, EPS revision: A-

  3. Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI), YOY ROE growth: 77.51%, EPS revision: A+

  4. Constellation Brands, Inc. (STZ), YOY ROE growth: 73.61%, EPS revision: A

  5. Equinix, Inc. (EQIX), YOY ROE growth: 55.67%, EPS revision: A+

  6. Nasdaq, Inc. (NDAQ), YOY ROE growth: 50.66%, EPS revision: A+

  7. MKS Inc. (MKSI), YOY ROE growth: 47.66%, EPS revision: A-

  8. Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. (KDP), YOY ROE growth: 44.74%, EPS revision: A

  9. US Foods Holding Corp. (USFD), YOY ROE growth: 43.69%, EPS revision: A-

  10. Keysight Technologies, Inc. (KEYS), YOY ROE growth: 40.99%, EPS revision: A+

data.title: Bullish sentiment sees a modest uptick, but investor mood stays cautious, shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Market sentiment among individual investors remained pessimistic overall, even as optimism improved slightly, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors.

Bullish sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will rise over the next six months, increased to 33.6% for the week ended April 1, up from 32.1% in the prior week. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment climbed to 51.4% from 49.8%.

Equity markets remained volatile through the week, as developments surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict continued to drive investor sentiment and risk appetite. Stocks saw sharp swings, with major indexes periodically falling into the red on concerns over potential escalation, before paring losses or rebounding on signs of possible de-escalation.

Uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz, and fluctuating ceasefire expectations kept markets on edge, while global equities reflected a broader risk-off tone across regions. Meanwhile, Treasury yields moved higher during the period, hitting multi-month highs and signaling growing pressure in fixed-income markets, adding to the cautious tone across asset classes.

Volatility remained elevated throughout the period, with investors navigating a mix of geopolitical uncertainty and shifting market expectations, which continued to weigh on broader confidence.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also fell during the week, with the figure standing at 15.0%, compared to 18.1% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Scorpio Tankers to invest $10M in nuclear propulsion venture
data.description:
Scorpio Tankers (STNG) up 3.9% in Thursday's trading after unveiling a strategic collaboration with reactor developer Ampera, backed by a $10M investment, to jointly develop and commercialize advanced micronuclear energy systems for marine, shipping, and related maritime applications.

The agreement aims to address growing global demand for reliable, zero-carbon power solutions in maritime, offshore, and port infrastructure markets, the company said.

Through the collaboration, Scorpio Tankers (STNG) said it will serve as the strategic market lead and domain expert for marine and maritime applications, contributing marine engineering, technical design, regulatory expertise, and global commercial relationships across shipping and maritime infrastructure markets, while Ampera will provide its microreactor technology, based on a compact, containerized design using thorium fuel.

"Nuclear microreactors have the potential to fundamentally change how ships and offshore infrastructure are powered, and we believe this collaboration positions us at the forefront of that transition," Scorpio Tankers (STNG) President and CEO Emanuele Lauro said.

data.title: Grifols facing restrictions in Canada after plasma donor deaths
data.description:
Canadian health officials imposed restrictions on all plasma centers run by Grifols (GRFS) after the Spanish company failed multiple regulatory inspections and following fatal adverse reactions in two plasma donors.

Regulator Health Canada said on Wednesday that a January inspection conducted at Grifols’ (GRFS) Canadian head office in Oakville, Ont., found that the company didn’t accurately evaluate donors’ suitability and lacked properly trained staff.

Grifols (GRFS), which runs 16 plasma collection centers across Canada, didn’t always comply with operating procedures and thoroughly investigate errors and accidents, a spokesperson for the regulator said, according to CBC.

Health Canada had issued noncompliant ratings following inspections at two other Grofols’ (GRFS) facilities in Calgary and Regina recently.

The fatal adverse reactions of two plasma donors in Winnipeg are still under investigation, the agency said, adding that it has yet to establish a link between plasma collection and deaths, which occurred in October 2025 and January 2026.

data.title: Chagee jumps after JPMorgan pitches the bull case
data.description:
Chagee Holdings Limited (CHA) rallied in Thursday afternoon trading after JPMorgan upgraded the premium tea stock to an Overweight rating from Neutral. Notably, Chagee's (CHA) valuation looks attractive to JPMorgan compared with the Yunnan, China-based company's China consumer peers. 

Analyst Jessie Xu highlighted that after same-store declines for five consecutive quarters, improvement was seen in Q4 of 2025, and the momentum extended into Q1 of this year. The firm believes the worst is behind the stock and that Chagee (CHA) should be able to realize positive same-store sales growth by the end of 2026. "We now see better risk-reward in Chagee and the potential for a rerating (P/E mean reversion) given the better chance of a turnaround supported by new initiatives, 8% FY26E dividend yield, and strong free cash flow," highlighted Xu.

JPMorgan's price target on Chagee (CHA) of $16 is based on a 10X 2027 P/E multiple, which was noted to still be below the historic mean. 

Shares of Chagee (CHA) rose 7.4% in Thursday afternoon action to $9.91. The IPO was priced at $28 per share, and the post-IPO high for the stock is $41.80.

data.title: Trump may impose tariffs on drugmakers who haven't signed MFN deals - reports
data.description:
President Trump, as soon as Thursday, may announce new tariffs on pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies that have yet to sign "most favored nation" deals with the federal government to provide cheaper medicines to Americans.

Bloomberg, the first to report the potential development, said that companies that haven't signed an agreement or aren't in negotiations will be subject to a 100% levy. The new tariffs are the result of a Commerce Department Section 232 Trade Expansion Act investigation which can lead to tariffs or restrictions on imported items to protect national security.

A draft of the plan viewed by Endpoints News provides some additional details: Pharmas that onshore manufacturing to the US will be hit with a 20% rate and would last four years; and if a company is impacted by more than one tariff, the lower rate will be implemented.

The pharma and biotech news website also said that products originating from some countries would only face a 15% tariff: the EU, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, South Korea, and Japan. The document also states that the UK would receive a 10% tariff that could eventually disappear, depending on pricing negotiations.

Also, active pharmaceutical ingredients produced in the US but manufactured into drugs outside the country would not face tariffs if they are eventually sold in the US.

Most major pharmaceutical companies have already signed MFN deals with the Trump administration. Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) is one that has yet to.

In March, the Midsized Biotech Alliance of America, which just launched in October, issued a statement opposing the president's "most favored nation" policy which it referred to as "importing foreign price controls."

The organization's members include Acadia Pharmaceuticals (ACAD), Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY), BioMarin Pharmaceutical (BMRN), Exelixis (EXEL), Incyte (INCY), and Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL).

data.title: Transocean adds $1B backlog with Norway and Brazil offshore rig contracts
data.description:
Transocean (RIG) up 2.1% in Thursday's trading after unveiling a new charter agreement in Norway and a pair of contract extensions in Brazil worth more than $1B in incremental firm contract backlog.

In Norway, the Transocean Barents semisubmersible was awarded a three-year contract with Var Energi (VARRY) at a $450K dayrate, with work expected to begin in mid-2027; the contract is projected to contribute ~$490M in backlog and includes options that could extend operations into 2034.

In Brazil, the Deepwater Orion was awarded a three-year contract extension through March 2030 with Petrobras (PBR) in direct continuation of its current activity, adding ~$420M in backlog, and the Deepwater Aquila secured a one-year extension with Petrobras through June 2028, contributing ~$160M in backlog.

Transocean (RIG), which is in the process of merging with rival Valaris to create the world’s largest driller, said it was awarded a $490 million contract by Vaar Energi in Norway for the harsh environment semi-submersible rig Transocean Barents.

The company also said it retired $358M in senior secured notes due 2028, using cash on hand and funds from a debt service reserve account; the move is expected to reduce interest expense by $39M over the remaining life of the notes.

data.title: Long-term mortgage rates hit their highest level in about seven months
data.description:
Long-term mortgage rates reached their highest level since early September of 2025, according to the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.46% as of April 2, up from 6.38% last week but below the year-ago level of 6.64%.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.77%, compared to 5.75% last week and down from 5.82% a year ago.

"With spring homebuying season in full swing, aspiring buyers should remember to shop around for the best mortgage rate, as they can potentially save thousands of dollars by getting multiple quotes," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.
